title: Effect of Grapes on Brain. Supplementing a high-fat diet with 1 percent grape powder was found to reduce high-fat diet-induced anxiety-like behaviors. Genetic expression analysis in the brain revealed distinct differences among diet groups, with 210, 360, and 221 uniquely expressed genes in the standard diet, high-fat diet, and high-fat diet plus 1 percent grape groups, respectively. Cluster analysis showed that the high-fat diet plus 1 percent grape group's gene expression profile was more similar to the standard diet group than the high-fat diet group, suggesting a partial reversal of high-fat diet-induced changes. These findings indicate that dietary grape consumption can benefit behavior and cognition, counteracting the negative impact of a high-fat diet. The observed improvements are associated with widespread transcriptional changes in the brain, highlighting the potential of grapes in modulating neurobiological responses to dietary stressors.

The observed improvements are associated with widespread transcriptional changes in the brain, highlighting the potential of grapes in modulating neurobiological responses to dietary stressors.
